reference_answer: BC-833014. This was the mistaken intake scan on 2026-04-04 and was superseded on 2026-04-08; it must not appear in current reports.
BAY-04-L (the left-side open shelving unit on the south wall) and BAY-04-I (the interior locker behind the bay's mesh door).
The iPhone. Its current label is EX-E-10.
No. EX-E-09 is the Samsung Galaxy; EX-E-10 is the iPhone. They are two physically distinct phones; only the visually adjacent numbering invites confusion.
The .38 revolver (EX-C-01) at BAY-04-L Shelf 3. Physical custody is confirmed; legal disposition is pending Judge Anwar's order, expected no earlier than 2026-05-20.
Folder labels EX-P-3 (financial papers, in BIN-A12) and EX-P-3B (correspondence, in BIN-A1-2).
TAG-R-9 belongs to the black canvas jacket EX-J-7 at SHELF-N-2; TAG-R-G belongs to the toolbox EX-T-2 at SHELF-N-Z.
LK-3 (ground floor) holds EX-U-1, the USB thumb drive; LK-03 (basement) holds EX-C-02, the currency bag of $4,200 USD.
SLIP-7720, issued for the rebagged Sony external drive (EX-D-04R).
CASE-22-O917 (with capital letter O) was corrected to CASE-22-0917 (with numeral zero) on 2026-04-15.
From 2026-04-04 (mistaken intake scan recorded as BC-833014) until 2026-04-08 (re-scan recorded as BC-883014). The packet treats those dates as the boundary events.
No. Sergeant Vidal flagged the pair for relabeling at the next intake cycle, but no relabeling occurred during this window. Exception B remains open.
Expected no earlier than 2026-05-20. The order has not yet issued at packet close (2026-05-04); only the earliest expected date is on record.
Eleven. The twelve active labels minus EX-D-04 (laptop, released 2026-03-12) leaves eleven items physically held.
